summary: DHH describes Basecamp's new feature for assigning responsibility on todo items and milestones. He initially worried it would shift the product from communication toward managerial control and blame assignment. After further discussion, he concluded the transparency benefits outweighed the risks, since all employees (not just managers) can see everyone's workload. The feature enables peer collaboration and gives employees a concrete way to push back on overloading.
key_points: New responsibility assignment feature was initially seen as conflicting with Basecamp's 'Project Management is Communication' philosophy
Making workload visible to all employees, not just managers, reframes the feature as a collaboration tool rather than a control mechanism
Transparent workload visibility empowers employees to push back against unreasonable task assignments
The team chose to accept the potential for misuse because the communication benefits were far greater
money_quotes: I saw this initiative pulling us towards the Control and away from Communication.
We made it so that every employee could see what any other employee had on their plate — not just the administrators (who are often managers).
If your todo list is already full, it's pretty hard as a manager to be oblivious to that fact and assign an even heavier load with your head in the sand.
If we need to get this done by Friday, maybe I shouldn't also be in charge of sending out 100 envelopes.
That fear of misuse was dwarfed by the communication potential.
key_insight: Transparency features that could enable managerial control become collaboration tools when visibility is given to everyone, not just managers.
